Foundations
Refined type scale, muted P&L palette & typefaces — shared across all directions
System
Design System

Hierarchy & type scale

Four distinct levels so the Month → Week → Day → Trade nesting reads at a glance. Notes are promoted to reading-weight prose; figures sit in tabular mono; labels in a small uppercase mono eyebrow.

Type scale
Eyebrow / label
Geist Mono · 10.5 · +0.16em
June 2026
L1 Month · Hanken 38 / 600
Week 2
L2 Week · Hanken 26 / 600
Thursday
L3 Day · Hanken 19 / 600
GLXG
L4 Trade · Hanken 15 / 600
The daily note reads like written prose, not a placeholder.
Body · Hanken 15.5 / 1.7
−$287.26
Figures · Geist Mono · tabular
Palette  ·  muted P&L
Background
base canvas
Surface
day card
Border
hairline
Ink
headings
Body
prose
Muted
meta

oklch muted

oklch muted
Typefaces
Hanken Grotesk
Humanist sans · UI & prose
Newsreader
Humanist serif · editorial dates
Geist Mono
Tabular figures · from your stack
D · Hybrid — where we're heading
Editorial layout (spine, prose, the day stat line you liked) rebuilt in sans, with Ledger's green/red edge, aligned figures, weekly bars & metric strip
Week view
The Journal  ·  Week 02

Week 2

June 8 – June 12, 2026
M
T
W
T
F

Two clean green days bookended a brutal Thursday. The math is simple: one oversized GLXG trade erased the entire week — that’s the lesson, not the eight small losers on Tuesday. Keep red days small and Thursday never happens.

Net P&L
−$478.64
Trades
31
Accuracy
60%
Profit factor
0.82

Monday

June 8
5 trades·63% win·PF 1.64·+$42.33

Clean open. Waited for the first pullback on NPT instead of chasing the spike — that patience set the tone for the whole session. Sized normal, took profits into strength, and walked away after the morning push. Exactly the day I want to repeat.

PatientFollowed plan
Trades  ·  55W  0L
NPT+$15.44
INHD+$14.71
+ 3 morenet +$12.17
NPTBest setupView trade →

Textbook green-to-red reclaim. Entered on the reclaim, added on the first pullback, trimmed half into the move. This is the A+ I keep talking about — patient entry, defined risk, let it work.

Tuesday

June 9
11 trades·49% win·PF 0.69·−$116.94

Choppy, headline-driven tape. I knew by 9:45 it was a “sit on your hands” day and traded it anyway. Eight small names just churned commissions, and CCTG was the real damage — I added to a loser instead of cutting because I’d decided it “had to bounce.”

ImpatientOvertraded
Trades  ·  115W  5L
PAVS+$23.84
XELB+$15.54
+ 8 morenet −$38.70
CCTG−$117.60
CCTGRevenge tradeView trade →

Took this right after the AHMA loss to “make it back.” Doubled size, no plan, moved my stop down twice. Worst trade of the week and it wasn’t close.

Wednesday

June 10
5 trades·68% win·PF 1.99·+$22.21

Quiet and disciplined. Cut my size after Tuesday — the right call. Took only the setups written in the morning plan, nothing heroic. Green and calm. Recovery starts with small wins like this one.

CalmFollowed plan
Trades  ·  55W  0L
DSY+$15.04
BATL+$3.88
+ 3 morenet +$3.29
DSYGood tradeView trade →

Patient entry on the opening drive — took the meat of the move and left the rest alone. Small, but exactly the process I wanted to see the day after Tuesday.

Thursday

June 11
6 trades·44% win·PF 0.24·−$484.34

Ugly. GLXG was a full-size position into a halt-prone runner with no clear stop — I had no business being that size. Two weeks of green undone in one afternoon. I need a hard daily max-loss that actually locks me out, not a number I can talk myself past.

FrustratedOversized
Trades  ·  61W  5L
ADIL+$14.65
LASE−$0.30
+ 3 morenet −$211.44
GLXG−$287.26
GLXGRule breakView trade →

Sized 3× my max on a halted name with no stop. Broke the one rule that actually protects the account. If I fix nothing else this month, I fix this.

ADILNeeds reviewView trade →

Small winner, but I held it twice as long as planned hoping it would carry the day. Right instinct, wrong reason — I was trying to dig out, not trade the setup.

Friday

June 12
4 trades·60% win·PF 1.42·+$58.10

Came in shaken but with a smaller plan and a hard stop written down before the open. Hit the stop once early and actually respected it. Ended green — but more importantly, ended in control. Closing the week down, not broken.

FocusedFollowed plan
Trades  ·  44W  0L
TXMD+$21.30
RILY+$18.44
+ 2 morenet +$18.36
Day view
←  Week 2  ·  June 8 – 12

Week view
The Journal  ·  Week 02

Week 2

June 8 – June 12, 2026

Two clean green days bookended a brutal Thursday. The math is simple: one oversized GLXG trade erased the entire week — that’s the lesson, not the eight small losers on Tuesday. Keep red days small and Thursday never happens.

31 trades·60% win·PF 0.82·−$478.64

Monday

June 8
5 trades·63% win·PF 1.64·+$42.33

Clean open. Waited for the first pullback on NPT instead of chasing the spike — that patience set the tone for the whole session. Sized normal, took profits into strength, and walked away after the morning push. Exactly the day I want to repeat.

PatientFollowed plan
NPT +$15.44INHD +$14.71SUNE +$8.36BYAH +$2.91PN +$0.90
NPTBest setupView trade →

Textbook green-to-red reclaim. Entered on the reclaim, added on the first pullback, trimmed half into the move. This is the A+ I keep talking about — patient entry, defined risk, let it work.

Tuesday

June 9
11 trades·49% win·PF 0.69·−$116.94

Choppy, headline-driven tape. I knew by 9:45 it was a “sit on your hands” day and traded it anyway. Eight small names just churned commissions, and CCTG was the real damage — I added to a loser instead of cutting because I’d decided it “had to bounce.”

ImpatientOvertraded
PAVS +$23.84XELB +$15.54AZI +$11.90YOUL +$3.00RGNT +$1.02GMEX +$0.00EPSM −$1.75QTEX −$10.42UK −$12.47AHMA −$29.98CCTG −$117.60
CCTGRevenge tradeView trade →

Took this right after the AHMA loss to “make it back.” Doubled size, no plan, moved my stop down twice. Worst trade of the week and it wasn’t close.

Wednesday

June 10
5 trades·68% win·PF 1.99·+$22.21

Quiet and disciplined. Cut my size after Tuesday — the right call. Took only the setups written in the morning plan, nothing heroic. Green and calm. Recovery starts with small wins like this one.

CalmFollowed plan
DSY +$15.04BATL +$3.88VSME +$1.85FLD +$1.33CHOW +$0.11
DSYGood tradeView trade →

Patient entry on the opening drive — took the meat of the move and left the rest alone. Small, but exactly the process I wanted to see the day after Tuesday.

Thursday

June 11
6 trades·44% win·PF 0.24·−$484.34

Ugly. GLXG was a full-size position into a halt-prone runner with no clear stop — I had no business being that size. Two weeks of green undone in one afternoon. I need a hard daily max-loss that actually locks me out, not a number I can talk myself past.

Sized 3× my max on a halted name with no stop. Broke the one rule that actually protects the account. If I fix nothing else this month, I fix this.

Small winner, but I held it twice as long as planned hoping it would carry the day. Right instinct, wrong reason — I was trying to dig out, not trade the setup.

Friday

June 12
4 trades·60% win·PF 1.42·+$58.10

Came in shaken but with a smaller plan and a hard stop written down before the open. Hit the stop once early and actually respected it. Ended green — but more importantly, ended in control. Closing the week down, not broken.

Month view — full nesting
Journal  ·  Month Review

June 2026

−$291.74

June started strong — the first week proved the plan works when I follow it. Then Thursday the 11th happened: one oversized, ruleless trade did more damage than two good weeks of edge. The whole month’s focus now is a hard, automatic daily max-loss. Process over P&L.

Trades
49
Accuracy
63%
Profit factor
1.05
01

Week 1

June 1 – June 5, 2026
+$186.90

A strong, patient week — proof the plan works when I follow it. Sized up only on the A+ reclaim setups and sat out the chop.

Monday
June 1

Strong open, took the gap-and-go I planned. Best day in two weeks.

ConfidentFollowed plan4 trades
+$88.20
Tuesday
June 2

Slower tape. One clean trade, then sat out the chop. Good discipline.

Patient2 trades
+$31.05
Wednesday
June 3

Gave back gains chasing a second entry that was never really there.

Chased5 trades
−$54.40
Thursday
June 4

A+ setup on the morning reclaim. Sized right and let it run.

FocusedBest setup4 trades
+$112.75
Friday
June 5

Tiny green into the weekend. Protected the week, no heroics.

Calm3 trades
+$9.30

Week 2

June 8 – June 12, 2026
−$478.64

Two clean green days bookended a brutal Thursday. The math is simple: one oversized GLXG trade erased the entire week — that’s the lesson, not the eight small losers on Tuesday. Keep red days small and Thursday never happens.

Monday
June 8

Clean open. Waited for the first pullback on NPT instead of chasing the spike — that patience set the tone for the whole session. Sized normal, took profits into strength, and walked away after the morning push. Exactly the day I want to repeat.

PatientFollowed plan5 trades
+$42.33
Tuesday
June 9

Choppy, headline-driven tape. I knew by 9:45 it was a “sit on your hands” day and traded it anyway. Eight small names just churned commissions, and CCTG was the real damage — I added to a loser instead of cutting because I’d decided it “had to bounce.”

ImpatientOvertraded11 trades
−$116.94
Wednesday
June 10

Quiet and disciplined. Cut my size after Tuesday — the right call. Took only the setups written in the morning plan, nothing heroic. Green and calm. Recovery starts with small wins like this one.

CalmFollowed plan5 trades
+$22.21
Thursday
June 11

Ugly. GLXG was a full-size position into a halt-prone runner with no clear stop — I had no business being that size. Two weeks of green undone in one afternoon. I need a hard daily max-loss that actually locks me out, not a number I can talk myself past.

FrustratedOversized6 trades
−$484.34
Friday
June 12

Came in shaken but with a smaller plan and a hard stop written down before the open. Hit the stop once early and actually respected it. Ended green — but more importantly, ended in control. Closing the week down, not broken.
